summary.largest_items: PVC S65, PVC Powder PM66R AGC

PP.sub: Sideways but heavy, exports lean on Asia
bullets: NSRP exported approx 6,000-7,000t homo to China and 8,000-9,000t to India this month.
Lotte yarn finalized 1,170 CIF; Jinneng China still high at 1,210; PP 114G ~33,200-33,500 VND/kg.
ICIS: Vietnam yarn/inj -20 to 1,150-1,190 CFR; regional random copo off 60-100 USD/ton.

PE.sub: Lotte sets market low, sentiment cools
bullets: Lotte Hyundai cut finalized LDPE to 1,300 CIF over the weekend; HDPE FL7000 at 1,180.
US-Iran peace deal and possible Hormuz reopening weigh on sentiment; PE seen easing further.
Domestic LDPE 40,000-44,500 with wide gaps; Chinese LLDPE the floor at 31,500 VND/kg.

PVC.sub: July offers pile in, domestic slides
bullets: SCG's July offer: SP660 765, SG660 775 CIF, down 55-60 vs last month (750 to large buyers).
AGC Vina cut domestic PM66R approx 2,500-2,800 VND/kg to ~21,816-22,248 incl. VAT.
Imports: Formosa S65 780, AGC K66 750-760, Sinopec/LG 700, Shintech 690 CIF.

editorial.changedNote: 23 of 50 moved down, led by PVC

summary: Lotte Hyundai cut finalized LDPE to 1,300 USD/ton CIF over the weekend — near the market low — with HDPE FL7000 at 1,180.
US-Iran peace deal and a possible Hormuz reopening are cooling resin sentiment; participants expect PE to ease further.
Domestic LDPE offers vary widely (44,000-44,500 North vs ~40,000 South clearance); Chinese LLDPE the floor at 31,500 VND/kg.
paras: Early last week, the offer price of LDPE Film LF512 from South Korea's Lotte Hyundai to Vietnam was announced at 1470 USD/ton/CIF; by the end of the week, the finalized selling price was recorded at 1400 USD/ton/CIF.

However, according to updates today, June 15, right on the previous weekend, Lotte Hyundai continued to lower the finalized selling price of LDPE Film for some large customers in Vietnam down to 1300 USD/ton/CIF, almost the lowest in the market and creating very strong competitive pressure. The finalized import price of Lotte Hyundai's HDPE Film FL7000 was also recorded at 1180 USD/ton/CIF, equivalent to Chinese BASF's HDPE Film.

Domestically, the offer price of LDPE among suppliers is recording a very large discrepancy. For example, with Chinese Petrochina's LDPE 2426H, today in the North, the popular offer price was recorded around 44,000-44,500 VND/kg/ with VAT; however, in the South, a seller was recorded "clearing stock" with a volume of about 100 tons at only 38,500 VND/kg/ excluding VAT, accepting sales at 40,000-40,500 VND/kg/ with VAT. This is currently the lowest price in the market for LDPE.

For LLDPE, "premium" items originating from the Middle East such as Sabic's 218WJ, Qamar's FD21HS... are still commonly traded around 33,500 VND/kg/ with VAT, moving sideways compared to the end of last week. According to market sources, many buyers bid the price down to below 33,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, but in reality, no seller has been recorded accepting sales at this price mark. The cheapest is still Chinese-origin LLDPE Film when recorded with transaction prices at 33,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, some buyers even revealed signing imports for Chinese Petrochina's LLDPE Film at 31,500 VND/kg/ with VAT...

The offer price level of various HDPE types is commonly fluctuating from 36,000-39,000 VND/kg/ with VAT; in which HDPE Film/blow originating from the US and China sit in the low-price segment, and not many sellers have been recorded adjusting prices compared to the end of last week...

According to market sources, the fact that the US and Iran have just agreed on a peace agreement (although both Washington and Tehran have not yet published the agreement text) and the possibility of reopening the Strait of Hormuz in the near future are having a relatively strong impact on the resin trading sentiment early this week. Most market participants assess that the prices of plastic resins in general, and PE in particular, are expected to cool down more clearly.

Especially in Southeast Asia, prolonged weak demand has reduced buyers' interest in imported goods. Meanwhile, the supply is improving, especially with more South Korean goods offered back to Southeast Asia, keeping the short-term outlook for PE still quite gloomy.

summary: ICIS put Vietnam PP yarn/inj at 1,150-1,190 CFR (-20); regional homo/copo/random all fell, random copo -60-100 USD/ton.
NSRP exported approx 6,000-7,000t homo to China (1,180-1,200) and 8,000-9,000t to India (1,260-1,280); Lotte yarn finalized 1,170.
Domestic mostly sideways and cautious: PP 114G ~33,200-33,500, T3034 ~34,000; Korean off-grade copo 30,500-31,500 VND/kg.
paras: According to ICIS pricing published on the last trading day of last week, June 12, the price level of PP yarn/injection in Vietnam last week decreased by an additional 20 USD/ton, down to 1150-1190 USD/ton/CFR. Broadening to the entire Southeast Asia and China region, prices of all PP homo, copo, and random types last week also decreased, with the decrease margin commonly from 10-40 USD/ton, PP random copo decreased sharply by 60-100 USD/ton.

In Southeast Asia, buyers' interest in imported PP was limited; stagnant end-product demand and weak domestic prices continued to put heavy pressure on purchasing sentiment. Many buyers have stocked up enough inventory to meet production for June and July, so they are hesitant to buy more, especially when perceiving that prices may continue to fall; meanwhile, some suppliers and traders still want to sell to clear their inventories.

Also last week, through a synthesis of market sources, large distributors of Nghi Son Refinery and Petrochemical (NSRP) Vietnam exported approximately 6,000-7,000 tons of PP homo to the Chinese market, with finalized prices ranging from 1180-1200 USD/ton/CIF. Some other sources said that, besides China, from the beginning of June until now, NSRP also exported approximately 8,000-9,000 tons of PP to the Indian market, with an average price of 1260-1280 USD/ton/CIF.

On the import side, today, June 15, some buyers shared that they received finalized import quotes for Lotte Hyundai's PP yarn at 1170 USD/ton/CIF. However, new offers from China remain cautious and keep prices relatively high. For example, for PP yarn, the offer price from a Jinneng distributor to Vietnam remains at 1210 USD/ton/CIF, higher than the aforementioned Hyundai-origin goods...

Not many import offers for PP from other origins have been recorded on the first trading day of the new week. However, information about the US and Iran having just agreed on a peace agreement (although both Washington and Tehran have not yet published the agreement text) and the possibility of reopening the Strait of Hormuz in the near future have led to assessments that prices of plastic resins in general, and PP in particular, will cool down more clearly.

Even so, in domestic trading today, not many sellers have been recorded adjusting PP prices compared to the end of last week. Market sentiment remains cautious, as both sellers and buyers want to further observe the price trend of the new week before deciding to adjust.

"For domestic PP plastic trading companies, most have cleared a relatively large amount of inventory recently and currently only hold a low level of inventory for rotation, applying a fast buy-fast sell tactic and accepting thin margins" – a source said.

In fact, late last week, in the North, some transactions for NSRP's PP 114G were recorded at 33,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, and even large-volume container buyers could finalize imports at a price of 22,800 VND/kg/ with VAT. This is almost the cheapest PP yarn item in the market at this time. Today, many commercial sellers still keep the offer price of PP 114G at a higher level, around 33,200-33,500 VND/kg/ with VAT.
BSR's PP T3034 goods last week also decreased very sharply to the level of 34,000 VND/kg/ with VAT. Entering the beginning of this week, offer prices from distributors mainly moved sideways.

Regarding PP copo and random, in the North, a distributor in the middle of this week offered South Korea Lotte's PP random copo J560s at 37,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, a sharp drop of up to 8,000 VND/kg compared to their own offer price in the middle of May. The offer price dropped deeply in the context where many other sellers continuously lowered prices to compete, and also because South Korean off-grade (OG) PP copo goods have lowered transaction prices down to only 30,500-31,500 VND/kg/ with VAT.

In the South, the selling offer price of PP copo (calculated generally for all origins) is commonly fluctuating in the range of 36,000-38,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, decreasing sharply compared to last week. South Korea Lotte's PP coating M9600 goods last week had the lowest offer at 48,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, but entering this week continued to decrease, with the lowest recorded finalized selling price at 46,000 VND/kg/ with VAT. Hyosung Vina's PP random originating from Vietnam is offered in the range of about 46,000-47,000 VND/kg/ with VAT...

Overall, although LSP has stopped production and only has a little PP copo inventory remaining (out of PP homo), our country's domestic PP homo supply is still in oversupply, because the production output from the remaining domestic plants remains strong. Import PP shipments from South Korea are gradually increasing again, keeping competition fierce.

summary: SCG offered July to Vietnam: SP660 765, SG660 775 CIF (-55-60 m/m), with finalized quotes as low as 750 to large buyers.
July import slate: Formosa S65 780, AGC K66 750-760 (740 for 1,000t+), Sinopec/LG 700, Shintech SE950 690 CIF.
AGC Vina cut domestic PM66R approx 2,500-2,800 VND/kg to ~21,816-22,248 incl. VAT; commercial oil-based PVC at 22,500-23,000.
paras: Late last week, Thailand's SCG announced offer prices for July PVC shipments to Vietnamese buyers. Accordingly, the offer price for SP660 is at 765 USD/ton/CIF, and the offer price for SG660 is at 775 USD/ton/CIF, which is 55-60 USD/ton lower compared to the finalized selling price last month. Some large domestic customers said they received finalized quotes from SCG at 750 USD/ton/CIF, however, they remain cautious and are not in a hurry to sign for large quantities.

Last week, several major manufacturers in Asia also offered July PVC to Vietnam. Among them, Formosa Taiwan offered PVC S65 at 780 USD/ton/CIF (subject to a 5% import tax); the offer price for PVC K66 from AGC Group’s two plants, Vinythai Thailand (AVT) and Ashahimas Indonesia (ASC), was at 750-760 USD/ton/CIF depending on the volume, with large purchases over 1000 tons offered at 740 USD/ton/CIF; the finalized import price of oil-based PVC from Sinopec, LG Chem, and Hygain China was recorded at 700 USD/ton/CIF; the finalized selling price of PVC SE950/ SE1000 from America’s Shintech was at 690 USD/ton/CIF...

Domestically, according to today's update, late last week, domestic manufacturer AGC Vina (ACVN) offered July 2026 PVC prices to the domestic market. The offer price for PVC PM66R is at 20,400-20,600 VND/kg/ excluding VAT for medium-to-small customers (depending on volume); the offer price is 20,200 VND/kg/ excluding VAT for the largest customers. Calculating the full 8% VAT, ACVN's offer price this month is around 21,816-22,248 VND/kg/ with VAT. Goods are delivered in the South. Compared to last month, ACVN’s PVC offer price this month dropped sharply by 2,500-2,800 VND/kg.

The remaining PVC manufacturer in Vietnam, TPC Vina, has not offered new shipment prices yet. However, in the commercial market, trading units with leftover inventories also continuously lowered prices to clear stock.

In the commercial market, today's selling offer prices for oil-based PVC of various origins (such as China's Sinopec, America's Shintech, or Thailand's SCG...) are commonly in the range of 22,500-23,000 VND/kg/ with VAT. Buyers are still only signing contracts moderately in small quantities because ACVN's domestic PVC price has dropped to 22,000 VND/kg/ with VAT, and also because the price outlook for July PVC is highly likely to continue decreasing as the rainy season approaches.

In the North, a distributor today also lowered the offer price for Thai SCG's PVC SG660 down to 23,600 VND/kg/ with VAT, a decrease of 400 VND/kg compared to last week, but trading remains very slow. Meanwhile, coal-based PVC SG-5 originating from China maintains its lowest offer price at 21,500 VND/kg/ with VAT, moving sideways or decreasing insignificantly compared to the end of last week...
